Description: Create the public src-layout package, pyproject, README, licence, ignore rules and CLI entry point.
Acceptance Criteria: Clean install on Python 3.11; `minibench-bot --help` works; no secrets or generated data tracked.

Description: Pin forecasting-tools 0.2.92 and test/runtime dependencies; add an explicit upgrade check.
Acceptance Criteria: Lock resolves on Python 3.11; installed package reports 0.2.92; CI fails on drift.

Description: Run unit/schema tests, formatting and type checks on supported Python versions.
Acceptance Criteria: Pull requests run deterministic offline checks and publish a clear pass/fail result.

Description: Validate config, required directories and provider credentials without printing secret values.
Acceptance Criteria: `verify-env` reports missing variable names only; exits non-zero on invalid live-submit settings.

Description: Create Pydantic settings matching config.example.yaml with safe defaults and cross-field validation.
Acceptance Criteria: Valid example loads after replacing model placeholder; invalid live-submit combinations are rejected.

Description: Document venv, config, environment variables, dry run and test commands.
Acceptance Criteria: A clean checkout can reach the offline dry-run fixture using only documented commands.

Description: Instantiate one client with configured base URL, token, timeout and pacing.
Acceptance Criteria: Client uses METACULUS_TOKEN and configured pacing; secret is redacted from logs.

Description: Fetch open questions with configured tournament ID and group-question mode.
Acceptance Criteria: Fixture and live-read modes return typed questions; current alias can be overridden without code changes.

Description: Persist normalized API snapshots for deterministic replay and tests.
Acceptance Criteria: Saved snapshot round-trips without network access and retains question/post/tournament IDs.

Description: Support smoke-test targeting without changing MiniBench config.
Acceptance Criteria: CLI can target `bot-testing-area` explicitly; production MiniBench remains unchanged.

Description: Map package question objects into a stable internal schema.
Acceptance Criteria: Golden binary, multiple-choice and numeric fixtures validate and retain resolution fine print.

Description: Preserve group parent identity while processing each subquestion as a forecastable record.
Acceptance Criteria: Unpacked fixtures produce one unique internal question per subquestion and no duplicate IDs.

Description: Flag date/conditional types as deferred instead of coercing or submitting.
Acceptance Criteria: Unsupported types create a diagnostic event and make zero model/submission calls.

Description: Normalize provider results with stable source IDs, URLs, timestamps, hashes and reliability tags.
Acceptance Criteria: Pydantic schema preserves original URL, published/retrieved times and raw artifact reference.

Description: Retrieve current and historical news while retaining article-level provenance.
Acceptance Criteria: Mocked call returns normalized documents; missing credentials fail before a paid call.

Description: Use Exa only when AskNews fails or when official-source/web retrieval is required.
Acceptance Criteria: Configured fallback records why it ran and preserves citations; no silent provider switching.

Description: Bypass general retrieval for configured official datasets such as FRED and authoritative resolution sources.
Acceptance Criteria: Router produces the same normalized document/observation format and records endpoint parameters.

Description: Canonicalize URLs, hash content and mark documents outside the question-specific freshness window.
Acceptance Criteria: Duplicate reports collapse without losing provenance; stale evidence is flagged deterministically.

Description: Save queries, provider configs, raw responses, costs and normalized documents.
Acceptance Criteria: Replay produces zero provider calls and the same research packet hash.

Description: Load prompts/forecaster.md, verify declared version and compute a content hash.
Acceptance Criteria: Every forecast stores prompt version/hash; changed bytes produce a new hash.

Description: Call one configured GeneralLlm model with a question-specific Pydantic response type.
Acceptance Criteria: Valid response returns typed output; malformed output gets at most one bounded repair attempt.

Description: Produce probability_yes plus attribution fields.
Acceptance Criteria: Golden output validates within configured bounds and includes base rate, adjustments and failure modes.

Description: Produce every exact option once with probabilities summing to one.
Acceptance Criteria: Unknown/missing/duplicate options fail validation; tolerance is 1e-6.

Description: Produce the nine declared percentiles for conversion by forecasting-tools.
Acceptance Criteria: Percentile levels are exact; values are finite, ordered and compatible with question bounds.

Description: Store provider response, parsed result, model settings and cost separately from canonical rationale.
Acceptance Criteria: Model replay makes zero API calls and reproduces the parsed forecast hash.

Description: Require prior/base rate, status quo, evidence links, load-bearing facts, failure modes and final output.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema rejects missing or unknown required fields and invalid source IDs.

Description: Apply binary bounds and exact multiple-choice normalization.
Acceptance Criteria: Boundary and sum tests pass; no arbitrary post-hoc renormalization is hidden.

Description: Use NumericDistribution.from_question and get_cdf to produce the submission array.
Acceptance Criteria: Exactly 201 monotone values for normal numeric questions; maintained PMF constraint passes.

Description: Flag or fail according to config before approval.
Acceptance Criteria: No-sources and stale-only fixtures produce explicit states and never pass silently.

Description: Create append-only forecast, research, document, approval, submission, resolution and score tables.
Acceptance Criteria: Fresh migration succeeds with WAL/foreign keys; unique version/idempotency constraints exist.

Description: Append one record per forecast version with parent linkage and canonical JSON.
Acceptance Criteria: Updating a question appends v2; v1 remains byte-identical.

Description: Persist validation, approval, submission and failure events in transactions.
Acceptance Criteria: Injected failures cannot leave an approved/submitted state without its event record.

Description: Create analysis-friendly derived exports for audit and polygraph use.
Acceptance Criteria: Exports round-trip record IDs/counts and never mutate SQLite.

Description: Exclude credentials and hidden chain-of-thought from persisted records/logs.
Acceptance Criteria: Fixture secrets do not appear in database, exports or logs; rationale remains concise/auditable.

Description: Approve or reject the exact forecast hash as append-only events.
Acceptance Criteria: Changed forecast invalidates prior approval; actor/timestamp/note are retained.

Description: Derive keys from tournament, question, forecast version and payload hash.
Acceptance Criteria: Same payload/key cannot create two attempts; changed payload requires a new approval/key.

Description: Return a deterministic receipt without any Metaculus write.
Acceptance Criteria: Dry run records payload/hash and makes zero HTTP post calls.

Description: Call public post methods after approval, capture exceptions and verify by refetch.
Acceptance Criteria: Success requires refetch confirmation; uncertain timeout blocks blind retry.

Description: Decide whether refetch receipt is sufficient or add a contract-tested narrow HTTP adapter.
Acceptance Criteria: Decision and test evidence recorded; no private package method dependency without a guard.

Description: Submit one explicit owner-approved test forecast and persist receipt/refetch.
Acceptance Criteria: Forecast appears on bot profile; ledger has approval, payload, attempt and verification snapshot.

Description: Process one current eligible question with human approval.
Acceptance Criteria: One live forecast succeeds once; complete pre-resolution record and verified receipt exist.

Description: Fetch outcomes/annulments and append resolution events.
Acceptance Criteria: Resolved fixture updates only via append event; annulled/ambiguous outcomes are not scored.

Description: Calculate Brier and log scores with versioned implementations.
Acceptance Criteria: Known examples match hand calculations; extreme probabilities are handled safely.

Description: Record platform numeric/log/peer scores instead of claiming an unverified local replica.
Acceptance Criteria: Numeric record identifies score source and does not label a proxy as Metaculus score.

Description: Group outcomes by domain, type, model, prompt and reasoning tags.
Acceptance Criteria: Export contains counts, calibration bins and score summaries with small-sample warnings.

Description: Add valid/invalid binary, multiple-choice and numeric records.
Acceptance Criteria: Fixtures cover all required fields, bounds and malformed outputs.

Description: Test fetch, post, timeout, 429/5xx and refetch verification paths.
Acceptance Criteria: No live network calls in CI; uncertain-outcome path blocks duplicate submit.

Description: Exercise one saved question through research/model replay to complete ledger record.
Acceptance Criteria: One command produces one validated record, zero provider calls and zero submission calls.

Description: Guard 201-point size, monotonicity, bounds and PMF steps against package upgrades.
Acceptance Criteria: Golden edge cases pass on 0.2.92; dependency drift triggers a visible failure.

Description: Document dry run, review, approval, submission, uncertain timeout and recovery.
Acceptance Criteria: Another operator can recover from each failure state without editing the database.

Description: Explain ledger lifecycle, immutable versions and polygraph export contract.
Acceptance Criteria: Every table/field and export version has an auditable definition.

Description: Create the Metaculus bot identity and store METACULUS_TOKEN securely.
Acceptance Criteria: Bot profile exists; token works in verify-env and is absent from repository/history.

Description: Choose an accessible current frontier model and set provider credentials.
Acceptance Criteria: Verified LiteLLM model name completes one structured fixture call within cost cap.

Description: Create AskNews API key and optional Exa fallback key.
Acceptance Criteria: verify-env recognizes keys; one citation-retaining test query succeeds.

Description: Read current rules and confirm bot/prize/payout requirements before live batch.
Acceptance Criteria: Eligibility, payout identity, update rules and admin deadlines are recorded in Verified Facts.

Description: Complete any survey or payout steps applicable to the entered tournament.
Acceptance Criteria: Required forms are submitted by the official deadline and evidence is recorded privately.
